cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Jennifer could have gone to the swimming pool. Let me go through each of the details step by step. First, the problem gives a series of events and times when other people saw Jennifer. The swimming pool is closed after 10pm, so she must have gone before that. Let me list out all the times she was seen doing other things to see what times are available for her to go swimming. 1. Jennifer woke up at 5am. So she starts her day then. 2. Richard saw her working at the office from 5am to 8am. So from 5 to 8, she's at the office. 3. Then Samantha saw her reading at the library from 12pm to 1pm. So from noon to 1pm, she's at the library. 4. Betty saw her buying a bike at the bike shop from 1pm to 2pm. So from 1 to 2, she's at the bike shop. 5. Sarah saw her buying a phone at the electronics store from 2pm to 9pm. That's a long time—so from 2pm until 9pm, she's there. 6. Thomas saw her walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. So from 9 to 10, she's heading there. 7. The swimming pool is closed after 10pm, so she must have gone before that. Now, let's see the timeline. Let me map out the times: - 5am to 8am: office - Then, there's a gap between 8am and 12pm. Wait, after 8am, what's next? The next event is at 12pm (library). So from 8am to 12pm, there's no mention of her doing anything. Is that a possible window? Then from 12pm to 1pm: library. Then 1pm to 2pm: bike shop. Then 2pm to 9pm: electronics store. Then 9pm to 10pm: walking to Statue of Liberty. After 10pm, pool is closed. So the possible time when she wasn't seen doing anything else would be between 8am and 12pm. Let me check if there's any other activity during that time. The next activity after the office is at 12pm. So between 8am and 12pm, she wasn't observed doing anything. That seems like the only window. Looking at the options given: (A) 8am to 12pm – this matches the gap between her office time and the library visit. (B) 9pm to 10pm – but during that time, she was walking towards the Statue of Liberty. (C) 2pm to 9pm – she was at the electronics store then. (D) 12pm to 1pm – she was at the library. Therefore, the only possible time she could have gone to the swimming pool is option A: 8am to 12pm. Also, the pool closes at 10pm, so going in the morning is fine. But wait, there's another thing to consider: if she went to the pool, would she have had time to do all the other activities later? Let me think again. If she went to the pool between 8am and 12pm, say, maybe from 9am to 10am, then she could still go to the library at 12pm. But the problem is asking between what times could she have gone, not the exact time. The question is looking for the range of possible times when she wasn't occupied.
